Ran `pylanceRunCodeSnippet` 
Completed with input: {
  "workspaceRoot": "c:\\Users\\rick\\projects\\Atlas",
  "workingDirectory": "c:\\Users\\rick\\projects\\Atlas",
  "timeout": 120000,
  "codeSnippet": "from pathlib import Path\nimport pandas as pd\nimport numpy as np\n\nrun_dir = Path(r'c:\\Users\\rick\\projects\\Atlas\\data\\output\\runs\\20260318_173935')\ndf = pd.read_csv(run_dir / 'eval_legs.csv')\n\n# keep settled legs only\nif 'hit' not in df.columns:\n    raise SystemExit('hit column missing')\ndf['hit'] = pd.to_numeric(df['hit'], errors='coerce')\nsettled = df[df['hit'].notna()].copy()\n\nfor col in ['p_adj', 'p_cal', 'p_role', 'p_for_cal', 'role_ctx_outs_used']:\n    if col in settled.columns:\n        settled[col] = pd.to_numeric(settled[col], errors='coerce')\n\n\ndef brier(p, y):\n    p = pd.to_numeric(p, errors='coerce')\n    y = pd.to_numeric(y, errors='coerce')\n    m = p.notna() & y.notna()\n    return float(np.mean((p[m] - y[m]) ** 2))\n\n\ndef logloss(p, y):\n    p = pd.to_numeric(p, errors='coerce').clip(1e-6, 1-1e-6)\n    y = pd.to_numeric(y, errors='coerce')\n    m = p.notna() & y.notna()\n    p = p[m]\n    y = y[m]\n    return float(-(y * np.log(p) + (1 - y) * np.log(1 - p)).mean())\n\n\ndef ece(p, y, n_bins=10):\n    p = pd.to_numeric(p, errors='coerce')\n    y = pd.to_numeric(y, errors='coerce')\n    m = p.notna() & y.notna()\n    p = p[m]\n    y = y[m]\n    bins = np.linspace(0.0, 1.0, n_bins + 1)\n    idx = np.digitize(p, bins, right=True)\n    idx = np.clip(idx, 1, n_bins)\n    total = len(p)\n    out = 0.0\n    for b in range(1, n_bins + 1):\n        mask = idx == b\n        if not mask.any():\n            continue\n        out += (mask.sum() / total) * abs(p[mask].mean() - y[mask].mean())\n    return float(out)\n\nsummary = {\n    'rows_total': int(len(df)),\n    'settled_rows': int(len(settled)),\n    'match_rate_on_eval': float(settled.shape[0] / len(df)),\n    'mean_hit': float(settled['hit'].mean()),\n    'mean_p_adj': float(settled['p_adj'].mean()),\n    'mean_p_cal': float(settled['p_cal'].mean()),\n    'brier_p_adj': brier(settled['p_adj'], settled['hit']),\n    'brier_p_cal': brier(settled['p_cal'], settled['hit']),\n    'logloss_p_adj': logloss(settled['p_adj'], settled['hit']),\n    'logloss_p_cal': logloss(settled['p_cal'], settled['hit']),\n    'ece_p_adj': ece(settled['p_adj'], settled['hit']),\n    'ece_p_cal': ece(settled['p_cal'], settled['hit']),\n    'role_ctx_used_share': float((settled['role_ctx_outs_used'].fillna(0) > 0).mean()) if 'role_ctx_outs_used' in settled.columns else float('nan'),\n}\n\nfor k, v in summary.items():\n    print(f'{k}: {v}')\n\n# show a small slice table for the worst stat/direction gaps under p_cal\nif {'stat', 'direction'}.issubset(settled.columns):\n    grp = settled.groupby(['stat', 'direction'], dropna=False).agg(rows=('hit', 'size'), hit_rate=('hit', 'mean'), pred=('p_cal', 'mean'))\n    grp['gap'] = grp['pred'] - grp['hit_rate']\n    print('\\nTop underpredicted slices:')\n    print(grp.sort_values('gap', ascending=False).head(5).to_string())\n    print('\\nTop overpredicted slices:')\n    print(grp.sort_values('gap', ascending=True).head(5).to_string())"
}

Ran `pylanceRunCodeSnippet` 
Completed with input: {
  "workspaceRoot": "c:\\Users\\rick\\projects\\Atlas",
  "workingDirectory": "c:\\Users\\rick\\projects\\Atlas",
  "timeout": 120000,
  "codeSnippet": "from pathlib import Path\nimport itertools\nimport pandas as pd\nimport numpy as np\n\nrun_dir = Path(r'c:\\Users\\rick\\projects\\Atlas\\data\\output\\runs\\20260318_173935')\ndf = pd.read_csv(run_dir / 'eval_legs.csv')\ndf['hit'] = pd.to_numeric(df['hit'], errors='coerce')\ndf = df[df['hit'].notna()].copy()\nfor col in ['p_cal','role_ctx_outs_used']:\n    if col in df.columns:\n        df[col] = pd.to_numeric(df[col], errors='coerce')\nstate = np.where(df['role_ctx_outs_used'].fillna(0) > 0, 'on', 'off')\ndf['state'] = state\nbase = pd.to_numeric(df['p_cal'], errors='coerce').clip(1e-6, 1-1e-6)\ny = df['hit']\n\ndef brier(p):\n    return float(np.mean((p - y) ** 2))\n\ndef logloss(p):\n    p = np.clip(p, 1e-6, 1-1e-6)\n    return float(-(y*np.log(p) + (1-y)*np.log(1-p)).mean())\n\nresults = []\nfor off_scale, on_scale in itertools.product([1.00, 1.01, 1.02, 1.03, 1.04, 1.05, 1.06], [0.94, 0.95, 0.96, 0.97, 0.98, 0.99, 1.00]):\n    p = base.copy().to_numpy(dtype=float)\n    mask_off = df['state'].eq('off').to_numpy()\n    mask_on = ~mask_off\n    p[mask_off] = np.clip(p[mask_off] * off_scale, 0.0, 1.0)\n    p[mask_on] = np.clip(p[mask_on] * on_scale, 0.0, 1.0)\n    results.append({\n        'off_scale': off_scale,\n        'on_scale': on_scale,\n        'brier': brier(p),\n        'logloss': logloss(p),\n        'mean_p': float(np.mean(p)),\n    })\nres = pd.DataFrame(results).sort_values(['brier', 'logloss'])\nprint(res.head(20).to_string(index=False))"
}

  "codeSnippet": "from pathlib import Path\nimport json\nimport pandas as pd\nimport numpy as np\nfrom Atlas.runtime.telemetry_calibration import TelemetryCalibration, apply_calibration_to_column\n\nroot = Path(r'c:\\Users\\rick\\projects\\Atlas')\ncalib = TelemetryCalibration.from_json(json.loads((root / 'data' / 'model' / 'telemetry_calibration.v2.json').read_text(encoding='utf-8')))\n\ndf = pd.read_csv(root / 'data' / 'output' / 'runs' / '20260318_173935' / 'eval_legs.csv')\ndf['hit'] = pd.to_numeric(df['hit'], errors='coerce')\ndf = df[df['hit'].notna()].copy()\n\n# The v2 loader expects p_adj as source and role_ctx_outs_used for role-state selection.\nscored = df.copy()\nscored['p_adj'] = pd.to_numeric(scored['p_adj'], errors='coerce')\nscored['role_ctx_outs_used'] = pd.to_numeric(scored['role_ctx_outs_used'], errors='coerce').fillna(0)\nscored['p_adj_src'] = 'p_adj'\n\ncal = apply_calibration_to_column(scored, calib, source_col='p_adj', out_col='p_new')\n\np = pd.to_numeric(cal['p_new'], errors='coerce')\ny = pd.to_numeric(cal['hit'], errors='coerce')\nmask = p.notna() & y.notna()\np = p[mask]\ny = y[mask]\n\nbrier = float(np.mean((p - y) ** 2))\nlogloss = float(-(y*np.log(np.clip(p, 1e-6, 1-1e-6)) + (1-y)*np.log(np.clip(1-p, 1e-6, 1-1e-6))).mean())\nmean_p = float(p.mean())\nmean_hit = float(y.mean())\nrole_on = cal['role_ctx_outs_used'].fillna(0) > 0\nfor state, m in [('off', ~role_on), ('on', role_on)]:\n    sub_p = pd.to_numeric(cal.loc[m, 'p_new'], errors='coerce')\n    sub_y = pd.to_numeric(cal.loc[m, 'hit'], errors='coerce')\n    mm = sub_p.notna() & sub_y.notna()\n    sub_p = sub_p[mm]\n    sub_y = sub_y[mm]\n    print(state, {\n        'rows': int(len(sub_p)),\n        'mean_p': float(sub_p.mean()),\n        'mean_hit': float(sub_y.mean()),\n        'brier': float(np.mean((sub_p - sub_y) ** 2)),\n        'logloss': float(-(sub_y*np.log(np.clip(sub_p, 1e-6, 1-1e-6)) + (1-sub_y)*np.log(np.clip(1-sub_p, 1e-6, 1-1e-6))).mean())\n    })\n\nprint({'rows': int(mask.sum()), 'mean_p': mean_p, 'mean_hit': mean_hit, 'brier': brier, 'logloss': logloss})"
}

The patched file did not help the actual v2 transform as written, so I’m re-optimizing against the true p_adj input rather than the already-calibrated run output.

1.0068408,\n            'PA|UNDER': 1.01, 'PRA|OVER': 1.0072742, 'PRA|UNDER': 1.01, 'PR|OVER': 1.0060664,\n            'PR|UNDER': 1.01, 'PTS|OVER': 1.0026367, 'PTS|UNDER': 1.01, 'RA|OVER': 1.01,\n            'RA|UNDER': 1.005196, 'REB|OVER': 1.0036611, 'REB|UNDER': 1.004859}}\n    ],\n    'notes': {'purpose': 'x', 'runtime_compatibility': 'x', 'audit_only_extensions': []}\n}\n\n\ndef eval_cal(cdict):\n    cal = TelemetryCalibration.from_json(cdict)\n    out = apply_calibration_to_column(df, cal, source_col='p_adj', out_col='p_new')\n    p = pd.to_numeric(out['p_new'], errors='coerce')\n    y = pd.to_numeric(out['hit'], errors='coerce')\n    m = p.notna() & y.notna()\n    p = p[m].to_numpy()\n    y = y[m].to_numpy()\n    brier = float(np.mean((p-y)**2))\n    logloss = float(-(y*np.log(np.clip(p,1e-6,1-1e-6)) + (1-y)*np.log(np.clip(1-p,1e-6,1-1e-6))).mean())\n    mean_p = float(np.mean(p))\n    return brier, logloss, mean_p\n\nmask_off = df['role_ctx_outs_used'].fillna(0).to_numpy() <= 0\nmask_on = ~mask_off\n\nresults = []\nfor off_scale, on_scale, k in itertools.product([0.90, 0.94, 0.98, 1.00, 1.02, 1.06], [0.90, 0.94, 0.98, 1.00], [0.92, 0.94, 0.96, 0.98, 1.00]):\n    c = copy.deepcopy(orig)\n    c['base']['k_shrink'] = k\n    for fam in c['families']:\n        scale = off_scale if fam['role_ctx'] == 'off' else on_scale\n        fam['mult'] = {kk: vv * scale for kk, vv in fam['mult'].items()}\n    brier, logloss, mean_p = eval_cal(c)\n    results.append({'k': k, 'off_scale': off_scale, 'on_scale': on_scale, 'brier': brier, 'logloss': logloss, 'mean_p': mean_p})\nres = pd.DataFrame(results).sort_values(['brier', 'logloss'])\nprint(res.head(30).to_string(index=False))"

Ran `pylanceRunCodeSnippet` 
Completed with input: {
  "workspaceRoot": "c:\\Users\\rick\\projects\\Atlas",
  "workingDirectory": "c:\\Users\\rick\\projects\\Atlas",
  "timeout": 120000,
  "codeSnippet": "from pathlib import Path\nimport json\nimport pandas as pd\nimport numpy as np\nfrom Atlas.runtime.telemetry_calibration import TelemetryCalibration, apply_calibration_to_column\n\nroot = Path(r'c:\\Users\\rick\\projects\\Atlas')\ncalib = TelemetryCalibration.from_json(json.loads((root / 'data' / 'model' / 'telemetry_calibration.v2.json').read_text(encoding='utf-8')))\nrun_dir = root / 'data' / 'output' / 'runs' / '20260317_152605'\ndf = pd.read_csv(run_dir / 'eval_legs.csv')\ndf['hit'] = pd.to_numeric(df['hit'], errors='coerce')\ndf = df[df['hit'].notna()].copy()\nfor col in ['p_adj', 'p_role', 'p_for_cal', 'role_ctx_outs_used']:\n    if col in df.columns:\n        df[col] = pd.to_numeric(df[col], errors='coerce')\n\n# score p_adj through current calibration v2\nscored = df.copy()\nscored['p_adj_src'] = 'p_adj'\ncal = apply_calibration_to_column(scored, calib, source_col='p_adj', out_col='p_new')\n\ndef brier(p, y):\n    p = pd.to_numeric(p, errors='coerce')\n    y = pd.to_numeric(y, errors='coerce')\n    m = p.notna() & y.notna()\n    return float(np.mean((p[m] - y[m]) ** 2))\n\ndef logloss(p, y):\n    p = pd.to_numeric(p, errors='coerce').clip(1e-6, 1-1e-6)\n    y = pd.to_numeric(y, errors='coerce')\n    m = p.notna() & y.notna()\n    p = p[m]\n    y = y[m]\n    return float(-(y * np.log(p) + (1 - y) * np.log(1 - p)).mean())\n\np_adj = pd.to_numeric(cal['p_adj'], errors='coerce')\np_new = pd.to_numeric(cal['p_new'], errors='coerce')\ny = pd.to_numeric(cal['hit'], errors='coerce')\nmask = p_adj.notna() & p_new.notna() & y.notna()\n\nsummary = {\n    'rows_total': int(len(df)),\n    'settled_rows': int(mask.sum()),\n    'mean_hit': float(y[mask].mean()),\n    'mean_p_adj': float(p_adj[mask].mean()),\n    'mean_p_new': float(p_new[mask].mean()),\n    'brier_p_adj': brier(p_adj, y),\n    'brier_p_new': brier(p_new, y),\n    'logloss_p_adj': logloss(p_adj, y),\n    'logloss_p_new': logloss(p_new, y),\n}\nprint(summary)\n\nif 'role_ctx_outs_used' in cal.columns:\n    role_on = pd.to_numeric(cal['role_ctx_outs_used'], errors='coerce').fillna(0) > 0\n    for state, m in [('off', ~role_on), ('on', role_on)]:\n        sub = mask & m\n        print(state, {\n            'rows': int(sub.sum()),\n            'mean_hit': float(y[sub].mean()),\n            'mean_p_adj': float(p_adj[sub].mean()),\n            'mean_p_new': float(p_new[sub].mean()),\n            'brier_p_adj': brier(p_adj[sub], y[sub]),\n            'brier_p_new': brier(p_new[sub], y[sub]),\n            'logloss_p_adj': logloss(p_adj[sub], y[sub]),\n            'logloss_p_new': logloss(p_new[sub], y[sub]),\n        })\n\n# by stat/direction on calibrated p_new\nif {'stat','direction'}.issubset(cal.columns):\n    grp = cal.loc[mask].groupby(['stat','direction'], dropna=False).agg(rows=('hit','size'), hit_rate=('hit','mean'), pred=('p_new','mean'))\n    grp['gap'] = grp['pred'] - grp['hit_rate']\n    print('\\nTop overpredicted:')\n    print(grp.sort_values('gap', ascending=True).head(5).to_string())\n    print('\\nTop underpredicted:')\n    print(grp.sort_values('gap', ascending=False).head(5).to_string())"
}

'off' else on_scale\n                fam['mult'] = {kk: vv * scale for kk, vv in fam['mult'].items()}\n            brier, logloss, mean_p = score(c)\n            results.append({'k': k, 'off_scale': off_scale, 'on_scale': on_scale, 'brier': brier, 'logloss': logloss, 'mean_p': mean_p})\nres = pd.DataFrame(results).sort_values(['brier', 'logloss'])\nprint(res.head(25).to_string(index=False))"

  "workspaceRoot": "c:\\Users\\rick\\projects\\Atlas",

  "timeout": 120000,
  "codeSnippet": "from pathlib import Path\nimport json\nimport pandas as pd\nimport numpy as np\nfrom Atlas.runtime.telemetry_calibration import TelemetryCalibration, apply_calibration_to_column\n\nroot = Path(r'c:\\Users\\rick\\projects\\Atlas')\ncalib = TelemetryCalibration.from_json(json.loads((root / 'data' / 'model' / 'telemetry_calibration.v2.json').read_text(encoding='utf-8')))\nrun_dir = root / 'data' / 'output' / 'runs' / '20260317_152605'\ndf = pd.read_csv(run_dir / 'eval_legs.csv')\ndf['hit'] = pd.to_numeric(df['hit'], errors='coerce')\ndf = df[df['hit'].notna()].copy()\nfor col in ['p_adj', 'role_ctx_outs_used']:\n    if col in df.columns:\n        df[col] = pd.to_numeric(df[col], errors='coerce')\nscored = df.copy()\nscored['p_adj_src'] = 'p_adj'\ncal = apply_calibration_to_column(scored, calib, source_col='p_adj', out_col='p_new')\n\ndef brier(p, y):\n    p = pd.to_numeric(p, errors='coerce')\n    y = pd.to_numeric(y, errors='coerce')\n    m = p.notna() & y.notna()\n    return float(np.mean((p[m] - y[m]) ** 2))\n\ndef logloss(p, y):\n    p = pd.to_numeric(p, errors='coerce').clip(1e-6, 1-1e-6)\n    y = pd.to_numeric(y, errors='coerce')\n    m = p.notna() & y.notna()\n    p = p[m]\n    y = y[m]\n    return float(-(y * np.log(p) + (1 - y) * np.log(1 - p)).mean())\n\np_adj = pd.to_numeric(cal['p_adj'], errors='coerce')\np_new = pd.to_numeric(cal['p_new'], errors='coerce')\ny = pd.to_numeric(cal['hit'], errors='coerce')\nmask = p_adj.notna() & p_new.notna() & y.notna()\nprint({'rows': int(mask.sum()), 'mean_hit': float(y[mask].mean()), 'mean_p_adj': float(p_adj[mask].mean()), 'mean_p_new': float(p_new[mask].mean()), 'brier_p_adj': brier(p_adj, y), 'brier_p_new': brier(p_new, y), 'logloss_p_adj': logloss(p_adj, y), 'logloss_p_new': logloss(p_new, y)})\nrole_on = pd.to_numeric(cal['role_ctx_outs_used'], errors='coerce').fillna(0) > 0\nfor state, m in [('off', ~role_on), ('on', role_on)]:\n    sub = mask & m\n    print(state, {'rows': int(sub.sum()), 'mean_hit': float(y[sub].mean()), 'mean_p_new': float(p_new[sub].mean()), 'brier_p_new': brier(p_new[sub], y[sub]), 'logloss_p_new': logloss(p_new[sub], y[sub])})"

I validated telemetry_calibration.v2.json against eval_legs.csv. The file parses cleanly, and the newest patch is a real improvement on the reconstructed run.

- Rows scored: 3,783
- Mean hit rate: 0.4253
- Mean `p_adj`: 0.3076
- Mean calibrated `p_new`: 0.4339
- Brier: 0.2389 -> 0.2240
- Logloss: 0.6821 -> 0.6384

Slice check:
- `role_ctx_outs_used == 0`: Brier 0.2197, logloss 0.6291
- `role_ctx_outs_used > 0`: Brier 0.2389, logloss 0.6709

So the latest calibration lifts the overall mean into the right neighborhood and improves both Brier and logloss, but the role-on slice is still the weaker part and is now slightly over-lifted relative to the observed hit rate.

'20260318_173935' / 'eval_legs.csv',\n]\n\nruns = []\nfor path in run_paths:\n    df = pd.read_csv(path)\n    df['hit'] = pd.to_numeric(df['hit'], errors='coerce')\n    df = df[df['hit'].notna()].copy()\n    for col in ['p_adj', 'role_ctx_outs_used']:\n        if col in df.columns:\n            df[col] = pd.to_numeric(df[col], errors='coerce')\n    runs.append(df)\n\n\ndef brier(p, y):\n    m = pd.to_numeric(p, errors='coerce').notna() & pd.to_numeric(y, errors='coerce').notna()\n    p = pd.to_numeric(p, errors='coerce')[m]\n    y = pd.to_numeric(y, errors='coerce')[m]\n    return float(np.mean((p - y) ** 2))\n\n\ndef logloss(p, y):\n    p = pd.to_numeric(p, errors='coerce').clip(1e-6, 1-1e-6)\n    y = pd.to_numeric(y, errors='coerce')\n    m = p.notna() & y.notna()\n    p = p[m]\n    y = y[m]\n    return float(-(y * np.log(p) + (1 - y) * np.log(1 - p)).mean())\n\n\ndef adjust_payload(payload, k_shrink, fam in p['families']:\n        scale = role_on_scale if fam['role_ctx'] == 'on' else role_off_scale\n        for key, value in list(fam['mult'].items()):\n            fam['mult'][key] = round(float(value) * scale, 6)\n    return p\n\nk_values = [0.74, 0.76, 0.78]\nunder_values = [0.97, 0.98, 0.99]\non_scales = [0.94, 0.96, 0.98, 1.00]\nresults = []\nfor k in k_values:\n    for u in under_values:\n        for s in on_scales:\n            payload = adjust_payload(base_payload, k, u, s)\n            calib = TelemetryCalibration.from_json(payload)\n            score_rows = []\n            for df in runs:\n                scored = df.copy()\n                scored['p_adj_src'] = 'p_adj'\n                cal = apply_calibration_to_column(scored, calib, source_col='p_adj', out_col='p_new')\n                p_new = pd.to_numeric(cal['p_new'], errors='coerce')\n                p_adj = pd.to_numeric(cal['p_adj'], errors='coerce')\n                y = pd.to_numeric(cal['hit'], errors='coerce')\n                m = p_new.notna() & p_adj.notna() & y.notna()\n                score_rows.append({\n                    'rows': int(m.sum()),\n                    'brier_new': brier(p_new[m], y[m]),\n                    'logloss_new': logloss(p_new[m], y[m]),\n                    'brier_adj': brier(p_adj[m], y[m]),\n                    'logloss_adj': logloss(p_adj[m], y[m]),\n                    'mean_hit': float(y[m].mean()),\n                    'mean_p_new': float(p_new[m].mean()),\n                })\n            total_rows = sum(r['rows'] for r in score_rows)\n            weighted_brier = sum(r['brier_new'] * r['rows'] for r in score_rows) / total_rows\n            weighted_logloss = sum(r['logloss_new'] * r['rows'] for r in score_rows) / total_rows\n            weighted_gap = sum(abs(r['mean_p_new'] - r['mean_hit']) * r['rows'] for r in score_rows) / total_rows\n            results.append((weighted_brier, weighted_logloss, weighted_gap, k, u, s, score_rows))\n\nresults.sort(key=lambda x: (x[0], x[1], x[2]))\nfor rank, item in enumerate(results[:12], 1):\n    brier_new, logloss_new, gap, k, u, s, rows = item\n    print(f'#{rank} k={k:.2f} under={u:.2f} role_on_scale={s:.2f} brier={brier_new:.6f} logloss={logloss_new:.6f} gap={gap:.6f}')\n    for idx, row in enumerate(rows):\n        label = '20260317' if idx logloss={row[\"logloss_new\"]:.6f}')\n"

  "codeSnippet": "from pathlib import Path\nimport json, copy\nimport pandas as pd\nimport numpy as np\nfrom Atlas.runtime.telemetry_calibration import TelemetryCalibration, ('20260318', root / 'data' / 'output' / 'runs' / '20260318_173935' / 'eval_legs.csv'),\n]:\n    df = pd.read_csv(path)\n    df['hit'] = pd.to_numeric(df['hit'], errors='coerce')\n    df = pd.to_numeric(cal['p_new'], errors='coerce')\n        role_on = pd.to_numeric(cal['role_ctx_outs_used'], errors='coerce').fillna(0) > 0\n        m = y.notna() & p_new.notna()\n        for state, mask in [('off', ~role_on), ('on', role_on)]:\n            sm = m & mask\n            rows.append((label, state, int(sm.sum()), float(y[sm].mean()), float(p_new[sm].mean()), float(np.mean((p_new[sm] - y[sm]) ** 2)), float(-(y[sm] * np.log(p_new[sm].clip(1e-6, 1-1e-6)) + (1-y[sm]) * np.log((1-p_new[sm]).clip(1e-6, 1-1e-6))).mean())))\n    total = sum(r[2] for r in rows)\n    agg_brier = sum(r[5] * r[2] for r in rows) / total\n    agg_logloss = sum(r[6] * r[2] for r in rows) / total\n    agg_gap = sum(abs(r[4] - r[3]) * r[2] for r in rows) / total\n    return agg_brier, agg_logloss, agg_gap, rows\n\nfor off_scale in [0.96, 0.98, 1.00]:\n    for on_scale in [0.90, 0.92, 0.94]:\n        payload = make_payload(0.79, 0.97, off_scale, on_scale)\n        agg_brier, agg_logloss, agg_gap, rows = score(payload)\n        print(f'off_scale={off_scale:.2f} on_scale={on_scale:.2f} agg_brier={agg_brier:.6f} agg_logloss={agg_logloss:.6f} agg_gap={agg_gap:.6f}')\n        for row in rows:\n            label, state, n, hit, pred, bri, ll = row\n            print(f'  {label} {state}: n={n} hit={hit:.6f} pred={pred:.6f} brier={bri:.6f} logloss={ll:.6f}')\n"
}

I’ve got the best cross-day compromise from the sweep. I’m generating the exact updated payload now so I can patch the live calibration cleanly instead of hand-tweaking 28 multipliers.

Ran `pylanceRunCodeSnippet` 
Completed with input: {
  "workspaceRoot": "c:\\Users\\rick\\projects\\Atlas",
  "workingDirectory": "c:\\Users\\rick\\projects\\Atlas",
  "timeout": 120000,
  "codeSnippet": "from pathlib import Path\nimport json\nimport pandas as pd\nimport numpy as np\nfrom Atlas.runtime.telemetry_calibration import TelemetryCalibration, apply_calibration_to_column\n\nroot = Path(r'c:\\Users\\rick\\projects\\Atlas')\ncalib = TelemetryCalibration.from_json(json.loads((root / 'data' / 'model' / 'telemetry_calibration.v2.json').read_text(encoding='utf-8')))\n\nruns = [\n    ('20260317', root / 'data' / 'output' / 'runs' / '20260317_152605' / 'eval_legs.csv'),\n    ('20260318', root / 'data' / 'output' / 'runs' / '20260318_173935' / 'eval_legs.csv'),\n]\n\ndef brier(p, y):\n    m = p.notna() & y.notna()\n    return float(np.mean((p[m] - y[m]) ** 2))\n\ndef logloss(p, y):\n    p = p.clip(1e-6, 1 - 1e-6)\n    m = p.notna() & y.notna()\n    p = p[m]\n    y = y[m]\n    return float(-(y * np.log(p) + (1 - y) * np.log(1 - p)).mean())\n\nagg_rows = []\nfor label, path in runs:\n    df = pd.read_csv(path)\n    df['hit'] = pd.to_numeric(df['hit'], errors='coerce')\n    df = df[df['hit'].notna()].copy()\n    for col in ['p_adj', 'role_ctx_outs_used']:\n        if col in df.columns:\n            df[col] = pd.to_numeric(df[col], errors='coerce')\n    df['p_adj_src'] = 'p_adj'\n    cal = apply_calibration_to_column(df, calib, source_col='p_adj', out_col='p_new')\n    p_adj = pd.to_numeric(cal['p_adj'], errors='coerce')\n    p_new = pd.to_numeric(cal['p_new'], errors='coerce')\n    y = pd.to_numeric(cal['hit'], errors='coerce')\n    m = p_adj.notna() & p_new.notna() & y.notna()\n    role_on = pd.to_numeric(cal['role_ctx_outs_used'], errors='coerce').fillna(0) > 0\n    print(label, {'rows': int(m.sum()), 'mean_hit': float(y[m].mean()), 'mean_p_adj': float(p_adj[m].mean()), 'mean_p_new': float(p_new[m].mean()), 'brier_p_adj': brier(p_adj, y), 'brier_p_new': brier(p_new, y), 'logloss_p_adj': logloss(p_adj, y), 'logloss_p_new': logloss(p_new, y)})\n    for state, mask in [('off', ~role_on), ('on', role_on)]:\n        sm = m & mask\n        print(' ', state, {'rows': int(sm.sum()), 'mean_hit': float(y[sm].mean()), 'mean_p_new': float(p_new[sm].mean()), 'brier_p_new': brier(p_new[sm], y[sm]), 'logloss_p_new': logloss(p_new[sm], y[sm])})\n    agg_rows.append((label, int(m.sum()), brier(p_new, y), logloss(p_new, y)))\n\nweighted_brier = sum(r[1] * r[2] for r in agg_rows) / sum(r[1] for r in agg_rows)\nweighted_logloss = sum(r[1] * r[3] for r in agg_rows) / sum(r[1] for r in agg_rows)\nprint({'weighted_brier_p_new': weighted_brier, 'weighted_logloss_p_new': weighted_logloss})"

Completed with input: {
  "workspaceRoot": "c:\\Users\\rick\\projects\\Atlas",
  "workingDirectory": "c:\\Users\\rick\\projects\\Atlas",
  "timeout": 120000,
  "codeSnippet": "from pathlib import Path\nimport json\nimport pandas as pd\nimport numpy as np\nfrom Atlas.runtime.telemetry_calibration import TelemetryCalibration, apply_calibration_to_column\n\nroot = Path(r'c:\\Users\\rick\\projects\\Atlas')\ncalib = TelemetryCalibration.from_json(json.loads((root / 'data' / 'model' / 'telemetry_calibration.v2.json').read_text(encoding='utf-8')))\nrun_ids = ['20260314_154511', '20260315_102437', '20260316_124824', '20260317_152605', '20260318_173935']\n\ndef brier(p, y):\n    m = p.notna() & y.notna()\n    return float(np.mean((p[m] - y[m]) ** 2))\n\ndef logloss(p, y):\n    p = p.clip(1e-6, 1 - 1e-6)\n    m = p.notna() & y.notna()\n    p = p[m]\n    y = y[m]\n    return float(-(y * np.log(p) + (1 - y) * np.log(1 - p)).mean())\n\nrows = []\nfor run_id in run_ids:\n    path = root / 'data' / 'output' / 'runs' / run_id / 'eval_legs.csv'\n    df = pd.read_csv(path)\n    df['hit'] = pd.to_numeric(df['hit'], errors='coerce')\n    df = df[df['hit'].notna()].copy()\n    for col in ['p_adj', 'role_ctx_outs_used']:\n        if col in df.columns:\n            df[col] = pd.to_numeric(df[col], errors='coerce')\n    df['p_adj_src'] = 'p_adj'\n    cal = apply_calibration_to_column(df, calib, source_col='p_adj', out_col='p_new')\n    p_adj = pd.to_numeric(cal['p_adj'], errors='coerce')\n    p_new = pd.to_numeric(cal['p_new'], errors='coerce')\n    y = pd.to_numeric(cal['hit'], errors='coerce')\n    m = p_adj.notna() & p_new.notna() & y.notna()\n    role_on = pd.to_numeric(cal['role_ctx_outs_used'], errors='coerce').fillna(0) > 0\n    off = m & ~role_on\n    on = m & role_on\n    row = {\n        'run_id': run_id,\n        'rows': int(m.sum()),\n        'mean_hit': float(y[m].mean()),\n        'mean_p_adj': float(p_adj[m].mean()),\n        'mean_p_new': float(p_new[m].mean()),\n        'brier_p_adj': brier(p_adj, y),\n        'brier_p_new': brier(p_new, y),\n        'logloss_p_adj': logloss(p_adj, y),\n        'logloss_p_new': logloss(p_new, y),\n        'off_rows': int(off.sum()),\n        'off_brier': brier(p_new[off], y[off]),\n        'off_logloss': logloss(p_new[off], y[off]),\n        'on_rows': int(on.sum()),\n        'on_brier': brier(p_new[on], y[on]),\n        'on_logloss': logloss(p_new[on], y[on]),\n    }\n    rows.append(row)\n    print(run_id, row)\n\nprint('median', {\n    'brier_p_new': float(np.median([r['brier_p_new'] for r in rows])),\n    'logloss_p_new': float(np.median([r['logloss_p_new'] for r in rows])),\n    'mean_p_new_gap': float(np.median([abs(r['mean_p_new'] - r['mean_hit']) for r in rows])),\n    'off_brier': float(np.median([r['off_brier'] for r in rows])),\n    'on_brier': float(np.median([r['on_brier'] for r in rows])),\n})\nprint('weighted', {\n    'brier_p_new': float(np.average([r['brier_p_new'] for r in rows], weights=[r['rows'] for r in rows])),\n    'logloss_p_new': float(np.average([r['logloss_p_new'] for r in rows], weights=[r['rows'] for r in rows])),\n    'mean_p_new_gap': float(np.average([abs(r['mean_p_new'] - r['mean_hit']) for r in rows], weights=[r['rows'] for r in rows])),\n})"
}

Ran `pylanceRunCodeSnippet` 
Completed with input: {
  "workspaceRoot": "c:\\Users\\rick\\projects\\Atlas",
  "workingDirectory": "c:\\Users\\rick\\projects\\Atlas",
  "timeout": 120000,
  "codeSnippet": "from pathlib import Path\nimport json, copy\nimport pandas as pd\nimport numpy as np\nfrom Atlas.runtime.telemetry_calibration import TelemetryCalibration, apply_calibration_to_column\n\nroot = Path(r'c:\\Users\\rick\\projects\\Atlas')\nbase = json.loads((root / 'data' / 'model' / 'telemetry_calibration.v2.json').read_text(encoding='utf-8'))\nrun_ids = ['20260314_154511', '20260315_102437', '20260316_124824', '20260317_152605', '20260318_173935']\nframes = []\nfor run_id in run_ids:\n    df = pd.read_csv(root / 'data' / 'output' / 'runs' / run_id / 'eval_legs.csv')\n    df['hit'] = pd.to_numeric(df['hit'], errors='coerce')\n    df = df[df['hit'].notna()].copy()\n    for col in ['p_adj', 'role_ctx_outs_used']:\n        if col in df.columns:\n            df[col] = pd.to_numeric(df[col], errors='coerce')\n    frames.append((run_id, df))\n\n\ndef make_payload(k, under, off_scale, on_scale):\n    p = copy.deepcopy(base)\n    p['base']['k_shrink'] = float(k)\n    p['base']['standard_under_penalty'] = float(under)\n    for fam in p['families']:\n        scale = on_scale if fam['role_ctx'] == 'on' else off_scale\n        for key, value in list(fam['mult'].items()):\n            fam['mult'][key] = round(float(value) * scale, 6)\n    return p\n\n\ndef brier(p, y):\n    m = p.notna() & y.notna()\n    return float(np.mean((p[m] - y[m]) ** 2))\n\n\ndef logloss(p, y):\n    p = p.clip(1e-6, 1-1e-6)\n    m = p.notna() & y.notna()\n    p = p[m]\n    y = y[m]\n    return float(-(y * np.log(p) + (1-y) * np.log(1-p)).mean())\n\nresults = []\nfor k in [0.77, 0.78, 0.79, 0.80]:\n    for under in [0.96, 0.97, 0.98]:\n        for off_scale in [0.96, 0.98, 1.00]:\n            for on_scale in [0.90, 0.92, 0.94, 0.96]:\n                payload = make_payload(k, under, off_scale, on_scale)\n                calib = TelemetryCalibration.from_json(payload)\n                briers = []\n                logs = []\n                gaps = []\n                weighted_rows = 0\n                weighted_brier = 0.0\n                weighted_log = 0.0\n                for run_id, df in frames:\n                    scored = df.copy()\n                    scored['p_adj_src'] = 'p_adj'\n                    cal = apply_calibration_to_column(scored, calib, source_col='p_adj', out_col='p_new')\n                    y = pd.to_numeric(cal['hit'], errors='coerce')\n                    p_new = pd.to_numeric(cal['p_new'], errors='coerce')\n                    m = y.notna() & p_new.notna()\n                    br = brier(p_new[m], y[m])\n                    ll = logloss(p_new[m], y[m])\n                    gap = float(abs(p_new[m].mean() - y[m].mean()))\n                    n = int(m.sum())\n                    briers.append(br)\n                    logs.append(ll)\n                    gaps.append(gap)\n                    weighted_rows += n\n                    weighted_brier += br * n\n                    weighted_log += ll * n\n                results.append({\n                    'k': k, 'under': under, 'off_scale': off_scale, 'on_scale': on_scale,\n                    'median_brier': float(np.median(briers)),\n                    'median_logloss': float(np.median(logs)),\n                    'median_gap': float(np.median(gaps)),\n                    'weighted_brier': weighted_brier / weighted_rows,\n                    'weighted_logloss': weighted_log / weighted_rows,\n                    'weighted_gap': float(np.average(gaps, weights=[int(pd.read_csv(root / 'data' / 'output' / 'runs' / run_id / 'eval_legs.csv')['hit'].notna().sum()) for run_id, _ in frames]))\n                })\n\nresults.sort(key=lambda r: (r['median_brier'], r['median_logloss'], r['median_gap'], r['weighted_brier']))\nfor i, r in enumerate(results[:15], 1):\n    print(f\"#{i} k={r['k']:.2f} under={r['under']:.2f} off={r['off_scale']:.2f} on={r['on_scale']:.2f} med_brier={r['median_brier']:.6f} med_logloss={r['median_logloss']:.6f} med_gap={r['median_gap']:.6f} w_brier={r['weighted_brier']:.6f} w_logloss={r['weighted_logloss']:.6f}\")\n"
}

Read [](file:///c%3A/Users/rick/projects/Atlas/src/Atlas/engine/new_probability.py#1248-1248), lines 1248 to 1248

I traced the live path end to end.

**What actually drives the slate-to-slate drift**

- In team_share_reallocator.py, the share matrix is rebuilt from only the recent window, then rotation players are defined by `min_rotation_games` and `min_rotation_avg_min`. That means the candidate pool itself changes when injuries and minutes patterns change.
- In team_share_reallocator.py, `build_teamshare_weights_for_outs(...)` assigns weights from the current out list and then applies a smooth reliability factor from `games_max / min_games_for_pattern`. If a pattern has weak support, its influence drops fast.
- In team_share_reallocator.py, `compute_role_multiplier(...)` blends three things:
  - trade-aware usage weights
  - teamshare weights
  - rotation reweighting
- The final multiplier is then reduced again by depth/capture logic and optional guardrails in team_share_reallocator.py.
- The engine writes `role_ctx_outs_used` from the `role_debug` payload in new_probability.py, and that field is what calibration uses to choose the `role_on` vs `role_off` family.
- There is also a separate blowout-sensitivity branch in new_probability.py. When `role_ctx_outs_used > 0`, it uses `0.85`; otherwise it uses `0.75`. That is a real source of day-level variance before calibration even runs.
- Calibration itself is a late overlay in orchestrator.py, and it only adjusts the final `p_cal` / `p_adj` surface after the engine has already decided the role context and blowout sensitivity.

**What this means**
- The model is not just “tuning differently” day to day. The active role pool, share support, and rotation weights are genuinely changing with the slate.
- That makes your median-over-five calibration choice sensible, because the downstream surface is structurally non-stationary.
- It also means the biggest remaining lever is probably not calibration alone, but the allocator path that decides `role_ctx_outs_used`, teamshare support, and the under-blowout branch.

**The most likely code-level pressure points**
- team_share_reallocator.py: recent-window share-matrix rebuild and rotation thresholds.
- team_share_reallocator.py: weak-support handling for out patterns.
- team_share_reallocator.py: final multiplier assembly, especially the blend/rotation/depth sequence.
- new_probability.py: the `role_ctx_outs_used` split also changes blowout sensitivity.

If you want, the next step I’d take is to tighten the actual code path instead of the calibration:
1. Reduce how much weak share-matrix support can move the final multiplier.
2. Make the `role_ctx_outs_used` branch less sharp so one extra outage does not flip the downstream behavior as hard.
3. Check whether the rotation reweight is over-amplifying new-slates with very different active-player pools.

User: absolutley. tighten the code paths and logic

GitHub Copilot: I’m making two focused logic changes: smoothing the role-context blowout branch so it scales with the number of outs, and making teamshare support budget stricter when support is only marginal.

Completed with input: {
  "workspaceRoot": "c:\\Users\\rick\\projects\\Atlas",

  "timeout": 120000,
  "codeSnippet": "import pandas as pd\nfrom pathlib import Path\n\npath = Path(r'c:\\Users\\rick\\projects\\Atlas\\data\\output\\runs\\20260309_143848\\eval_legs.csv')\ndf = pd.read_csv(path)\n\n# normalize columns we care about\ncols = {\n    'role_ctx_outs_used': 'role_ctx_outs_used',\n    'role_ctx_mult': 'role_ctx_mult',\n    'p_role': 'p_role',\n    'p_adj': 'p_adj',\n    'role_ctx_reason': 'role_ctx_reason',\n    'role_ctx_components': 'role_ctx_components',\n    'role_ctx_component_mults': 'role_ctx_component_mults',\n    'role_ctx_team': 'role_ctx_team',\n    'role_ctx_stat': 'role_ctx_stat',\n    'eval_match_quality': 'eval_match_quality',\n}\nfor c in list(cols):\n    if c not in df.columns:\n        print(f'missing {c}')\n\nou = pd.to_numeric(df['role_ctx_outs_used'], errors='coerce').fillna(0.0)\nmult = pd.to_numeric(df['role_ctx_mult'], errors='coerce')\npp = pd.to_numeric(df['p_adj'], errors='coerce') - pd.to_numeric(df['p_role'], errors='coerce')\n\nsummary = {\n    'rows': int(len(df)),\n    'outs_used_rows': int((ou > 0).sum()),\n    'outs_used_share': float((ou > 0).mean()),\n    'outs_used_mean': float(ou.mean()),\n    'outs_used_p90': float(ou.quantile(0.90)),\n    'outs_used_max': float(ou.max()),\n    'role_mult_mean': float(mult.mean()),\n    'role_mult_med': float(mult.median()),\n    'role_mult_p05': float(mult.quantile(0.05)),\n    'role_mult_p95': float(mult.quantile(0.95)),\n    'role_mult_gt1_share': float((mult > 1.0).mean()),\n    'role_mult_lt1_share': float((mult < 1.0).mean()),\n    'delta_p_adj_minus_p_role_mean': float(pp.mean()),\n    'delta_p_adj_minus_p_role_med': float(pp.median()),\n    'delta_p_adj_minus_p_role_p95': float(pp.quantile(0.95)),\n}\nprint('SUMMARY')\nfor k, v in summary.items():\n    print(f'{k}: {v}')\n\nprint('\\nBY STAT')\nby_stat = (\n    df.assign(ou=ou, mult=mult, delta=pp)\n      .groupby('role_ctx_stat', dropna=False)\n      .agg(\n          rows=('role_ctx_stat', 'size'),\n          outs_used_share=('ou', lambda s: float((s > 0).mean())),\n          outs_used_mean=('ou', 'mean'),\n          mult_mean=('mult', 'mean'),\n          mult_med=('mult', 'median'),\n          delta_mean=('delta', 'mean'),\n          delta_med=('delta', 'median'),\n      )\n      .sort_values('outs_used_share', ascending=False)\n)\nprint(by_stat.to_string())\n\nprint('\\nTOP ROLE_REASONS')\nprint(df['role_ctx_reason'].astype(str).value_counts().head(15).to_string())\n\n# rows where role ctx actually moved p materially\nmoved = df.loc[(ou > 0) & (pp.abs() >= 0.02), ['player', 'team', 'role_ctx_stat', 'role_ctx_reason', 'role_ctx_outs_used', 'role_ctx_mult', 'p_role', 'p_adj']]\nprint('\\nMOVED_ROWS', len(moved))\nprint(moved.head(20).to_string(index=False))\n"

Completed with input: {
  "workspaceRoot": "c:\\Users\\rick\\projects\\Atlas",

  "codeSnippet": "import pandas as pd\nfrom pathlib import Path\n\npath = Path(r'c:\\Users\\rick\\projects\\Atlas\\data\\output\\runs\\20260309_143848\\eval_legs.csv')\ndf = pd.read_csv(path)\nou = pd.to_numeric(df['role_ctx_outs_used'], errors='coerce').fillna(0.0)\nmult = pd.to_numeric(df['role_ctx_mult'], errors='coerce')\ndelta = pd.to_numeric(df['p_adj'], errors='coerce') - pd.to_numeric(df['p_role'], errors='coerce')\n\n# aggregate by player/team/stat to find the heaviest allocation effects\nagg = (\n    df.assign(ou=ou, mult=mult, delta=delta)\n      .groupby(['team', 'player', 'role_ctx_stat'], dropna=False)\n      .agg(\n          rows=('player', 'size'),\n          ou_mean=('ou', 'mean'),\n          ou_max=('ou', 'max'),\n          mult_mean=('mult', 'mean'),\n          mult_max=('mult', 'max'),\n          delta_mean=('delta', 'mean'),\n          delta_min=('delta', 'min'),\n          delta_max=('delta', 'max'),\n      )\n      .reset_index()\n)\n\nprint('TOP BY OUTS USED')\nprint(agg.sort_values(['ou_max', 'mult_max'], ascending=[False, False]).head(15).to_string(index=False))\n\nprint('\\nTOP BY MULTIPLIER')\nprint(agg.sort_values(['mult_max', 'ou_max'], ascending=[False, False]).head(15).to_string(index=False))\n\nprint('\\nROWS WITH MULT >= 1.10')\nsubset = df.loc[mult >= 1.10, ['player', 'team', 'role_ctx_stat', 'role_ctx_reason', 'role_ctx_outs_used', 'role_ctx_mult', 'p_role', 'p_adj']]\nprint(int(len(subset)))\nprint(subset.sort_values(['role_ctx_mult', 'role_ctx_outs_used'], ascending=[False, False]).head(25).to_string(index=False))\n"
}

I pulled the allocator behavior into concrete numbers from the current run outputs.

- The allocator logic lives in team_share_reallocator.py and now does three important things: smooths teamshare reliability instead of cliffing at a hard minimum, sharpens usage weights, and blends teamshare with usage using support-aware scaling.
- The run reconstruction for data/output/runs/20260309_143848/eval_legs_reconstruction_report.json is healthy: 2,533 rows total, 2,189 matched, 344 unmatched, for an 86.4% match rate.
- On data/output/runs/20260309_143848/eval_legs.csv, the role allocator fired on 205 rows, about 8.1% of the slate.
- The average role multiplier was 1.0108, median 1.0, and 95th percentile 1.1377.
- When role context fired, the average p_adj minus p_role shift was -0.0729 overall, with 183 rows moving by at least 0.02.

Where it mattered most:

- The strongest effects are concentrated on OKC.
- Highest multipliers by player were Cason Wallace at 1.191, Shai Gilgeous-Alexander at 1.184, Luguentz Dort at 1.182, Chet Holmgren at 1.163, and Jaylin Williams at 1.147.
- The most active rows by outs used were also OKC-heavy, with Luguentz Dort, Cason Wallace, Shai Gilgeous-Alexander, Chet Holmgren, and Jaylin Williams leading.
- Most rows still fell into no_outs or combo_no_effect, which means the allocator is selective rather than broadly inflating everything.

The key read on the change is this: the allocator is now clearly active, but the actual impact is concentrated on supported out patterns, especially on the OKC slate. It is not broadly moving the whole board.

Completed with input: {

  "codeSnippet": "import pandas as pd\nfrom pathlib import Path\n\nruns = [\n    '20260308_105308',\n    '20260309_143848',\n    '20260310_070252',\n]\nbase = Path(r'c:\\Users\\rick\\projects\\Atlas\\data\\output\\runs')\n\nsummaries = []\nfor run in runs:\n    path = base / run / 'eval_legs.csv'\n    df = pd.read_csv(path)\n    ou = pd.to_numeric(df['role_ctx_outs_used'], errors='coerce').fillna(0.0)\n    mult = pd.to_numeric(df['role_ctx_mult'], errors='coerce')\n    delta = pd.to_numeric(df['p_adj'], errors='coerce') - pd.to_numeric(df['p_role'], errors='coerce')\n    summaries.append({\n        'run': run,\n        'rows': len(df),\n        'outs_used_rows': int((ou > 0).sum()),\n        'outs_used_share': float((ou > 0).mean()),\n        'outs_used_mean': float(ou.mean()),\n        'outs_used_max': float(ou.max()),\n        'role_mult_mean': float(mult.mean()),\n        'role_mult_med': float(mult.median()),\n        'role_mult_p95': float(mult.quantile(0.95)),\n        'role_mult_gt1_share': float((mult > 1.0).mean()),\n        'delta_mean': float(delta.mean()),\n        'delta_med': float(delta.median()),\n        'delta_p95': float(delta.quantile(0.95)),\n        'delta_abs_mean': float(delta.abs().mean()),\n    })\n\nsummary_df = pd.DataFrame(summaries)\nprint('RUN_SUMMARIES')\nprint(summary_df.to_string(index=False))\n\n# pairwise deltas relative to 20260308_105308\nanchor = summary_df.set_index('run').loc['20260308_105308']\nfor run in ['20260309_143848', '20260310_070252']:\n    row = summary_df.set_index('run').loc[run]\n    print(f'\\nDELTA vs 20260308_105308 -> {run}')\n    for col in ['outs_used_share', 'outs_used_mean', 'outs_used_max', 'role_mult_mean', 'role_mult_med', 'role_mult_p95', 'role_mult_gt1_share', 'delta_mean', 'delta_med', 'delta_abs_mean']:\n        print(f'{col}: {row[col] - anchor[col]:.6f}')\n\n# stat breakdown for the main run only\nmain = pd.read_csv(base / '20260309_143848' / 'eval_legs.csv')\nou = pd.to_numeric(main['role_ctx_outs_used'], errors='coerce').fillna(0.0)\nmult = pd.to_numeric(main['role_ctx_mult'], errors='coerce')\ndelta = pd.to_numeric(main['p_adj'], errors='coerce') - pd.to_numeric(main['p_role'], errors='coerce')\nby_stat = (\n    main.assign(ou=ou, mult=mult, delta=delta)\n        .groupby('role_ctx_stat', dropna=False)\n        .agg(\n            rows=('role_ctx_stat', 'size'),\n            outs_used_share=('ou', lambda s: float((s > 0).mean())),\n            outs_used_mean=('ou', 'mean'),\n            mult_mean=('mult', 'mean'),\n            mult_med=('mult', 'median'),\n            delta_mean=('delta', 'mean'),\n            delta_med=('delta', 'median'),\n        )\n        .sort_values('outs_used_share', ascending=False)\n)\nprint('\\nMAIN_BY_STAT')\nprint(by_stat.to_string())\n"

pd.to_numeric(main['p_role'], errors='coerce')\nby_team = (\n    main.assign(ou=ou, mult=mult, delta=delta)\n        .groupby('role_ctx_team', dropna=False)\n        .agg(\n            rows=('role_ctx_team', 'size'),\n            outs_used_share=('ou', lambda s: float((s > 0).mean())),\n            outs_used_mean=('ou', 'mean'),\n            mult_mean=('mult', 'mean'),\n            mult_med=('mult', 'median'),\n            delta_mean=('delta', 'mean'),\n            delta_med=('delta', 'median'),\n        )\n        .sort_values('outs_used_share', ascending=False)\n)\nprint('\\nMAIN_BY_TEAM')\nprint(by_team.head(20).to_string())\n\nby_stat = (\n    main.assign(ou=ou, mult=mult, delta=delta)\n        .groupby('role_ctx_stat', dropna=False)\n        .agg(\n            rows=('role_ctx_stat', 'size'),\n            outs_used_share=('ou', lambda s: float((s > 0).mean())),\n            outs_used_mean=('ou', 'mean'),\n            mult_mean=('mult', 'mean'),\n            mult_med=('mult', 'median'),\n            delta_mean=('delta', 'mean'),\n            delta_med=('delta', 'median'),\n        )\n        .sort_values('outs_used_share', ascending=False)\n)\nprint('\\nMAIN_BY_STAT')\nprint(by_stat.to_string())\n\n# top player-team combinations by multiplier and outs used\nagg = (\n    main.assign(ou=ou, mult=mult, delta=delta)\n        .groupby(['team', 'player', 'role_ctx_stat'], dropna=False)\n        .agg(rows=('player', 'size'), ou_max=('ou', 'max'), mult_max=('mult', 'max'), delta_mean=('delta', 'mean'))\n        .reset_index()\n)\nprint('\\nTOP_BY_OUTS_USED')\nprint(agg.sort_values(['ou_max', 'mult_max'], ascending=[False, False]).head(12).to_string(index=False))\nprint('\\nTOP_BY_MULTIPLIER')\nprint(agg.sort_values(['mult_max', 'ou_max'], ascending=[False, False]).head(12).to_string(index=False))\n"
}

- `OKC` dominated allocator activity in the 20260309 run.

**Share-matrix issue and fix**
- While inspecting the support table generation path, I found a cleanup bug in build_share_matrix.py.
- The zero-weight and low-support cleanup logic was accidentally nested so it only ran when `games` was missing.
- I fixed that by dedenting the cleanup so it always runs after `games` normalization.
- After regeneration, share_matrix.csv was clean:
  - no rows with `games < 3`
  - no zero-weight rows
  - no near-zero leakage

- The allocator numbers from the 20260309 comparison were computed before the share-matrix cleanup fix.
- So those results are useful as a pre-fix reference, but they are not the final post-fix allocator numbers.

**Summary**

The thread moved from a stale-log concern into allocator-change telemetry, then into repeated same-snapshot reruns to isolate why support-backed outs kept disappearing.

1. The original focus was a run-folder issue around 20260309_143848, but you redirected away from missing logs and toward the allocator change results.
2. We then treated the same snapshot as a control and reran it repeatedly, comparing fresh evals against the original control run instead of chasing older logs.
3. The allocator path was traced through build_share_matrix.py, team_share_reallocator.py, and oracle_tuner.py.
4. A share-matrix cleanup issue had already been fixed earlier, but the reruns still looked too conservative.

**Most recent debugging phase**

The last major discovery was that the sandbox replay was not actually seeing IAEL invalidations at launch.

1. I patched replay_scenario.py so the sandbox run seeds both injury_invalidations_latest.json and status_latest.json before engine startup.
2. I also patched new_probability.py so the IAEL/status fallback respects sandbox paths instead of only the repo-level dashboard file.
3. Inspecting main.py confirmed the engine really loads injury_invalidations_latest.json, which explained why seeding only status_latest.json did not work.

**What the latest runs showed**

Before the IAEL fix, the audit outputs still looked like fallback mode:

1. oracle_20260319_115706.json and oracle_20260319_120357.json still showed `role_ctx_reason = "no_iael"` on sampled rows.
2. After the replay seeding fix, oracle_20260319_121123.json showed the engine was finally IAEL-aware, with `role_ctx_reason = "ok"` on sampled legs.

A direct row comparison after that fix showed the allocator path is now behaving much more like the control on at least some legs:

1. Control run example from scored_legs_deduped.csv:
   - Brandon Miller, CHA, AST, OVER
   - `p = 0.667183`
   - `p_role = 0.698580`
   - `p_adj = 0.650651`
   - `role_ctx_mult = 1.051543`
   - `role_ctx_mult_raw = 1.066227`
   - `role_ctx_outs_used = 1`
2. Latest rerun example from scored_legs_deduped.csv:
   - Same player/stat/direction
   - `p = 0.667183`
   - `p_role = 0.699480`
   - `p_adj = 0.651490`
   - `role_ctx_mult = 1.053982`
   - `role_ctx_mult_raw = 1.069908`
   - `role_ctx_outs_used = 1`

So the current state is no longer “IAEL missing entirely.” The remaining issue is narrower: some legs still show zero outs-used support, while others now match the control more closely.

**Important recent command behavior**

1. I first tried a compact Python one-liner to compare control vs current, but it failed with a `SyntaxError` because the inline command was too compressed.
2. I retried with a simpler direct compare and found that a naïve choice like Bub Carrington, WAS, PTS, OVER was not a useful control-support leg because it showed `role_ctx_outs_used = 0` in both runs.
3. I then searched the control run for a row with `role_ctx_outs_used > 0` and used Brandon Miller, CHA, AST, OVER as the comparison target.
4. That comparison succeeded and showed the rerun is now producing support on the same leg, which is the best signal so far that the replay plumbing is fixed.

**Bottom line**

User: Are you saying they showed as out on the injury report in the IAEL but there is no support during the run? 

GitHub Copilot: Yes, that is the distinction.

The latest rerun is now seeing the IAEL input, so the problem is not “the player was never marked out.” The issue is:

- the injury invalidation data is present during the run
- but some legs still end up with role_ctx_outs_used = 0
- meaning the allocator did not actually assign support for those legs

So the current diagnosis is not “IAEL missing,” it is “IAEL is loaded, but support is not consistently materializing.”

One important nuance: not every leg behaves the same. At least one control-supported leg, Brandon Miller on CHA AST OVER, now matches support in the rerun. That tells us the runtime plumbing is working, and the remaining failures are in the support-matching or thresholding path, not in whether the player was listed in IAEL at all.

User: It depends on what player was actually out in the IAEL report. If it was a player who did not produce many stats or played less than a certain amount of minutes there will be no support for the other team players. However, if Julius randle is out the other players should absolutley get other support. It also depends on what run you are choosing, I did have some issues some time ago but if it was recent within the last 2 weeks it will be accurate IAEL and injury info

GitHub Copilot: Yes, that’s the right framing.

What matters is not just whether a player appears in IAEL, but whether that out/invalidation actually translates into a supportable pattern for the other players in that run. So:

- if the out player has low minutes, low stats, or doesn’t create a strong usable pattern, support can legitimately be absent
- if Julius Randle is out, you’d generally expect other players on that team to pick up support, assuming the share-matrix keys and thresholds line up
- recent runs, especially within the last 2 weeks, should be the accurate IAEL/injury data if the replay plumbing is using the current files

That matches what we just saw: IAEL is loading correctly now, but support still varies by specific leg rather than appearing everywhere automatically.

User: Well each IAEL injury report will be specific to that day. Some players may not be "injured" they may be out on rest that day. You can have the share matrix literally look %100 different at the beginning of a day and before game times depending on who is out for that day

GitHub Copilot: Yes. That is exactly why the same snapshot has to be evaluated against the same day-specific IAEL state.

What you’re describing means:

- the injury report is not a static roster truth
- it can include rest, late scratches, and other day-specific exclusions
- the share matrix can shift a lot based on who is out that day
- early in the day versus right before lock, the support pattern can be materially different

So a run is only comparable if we know:

1. which IAEL/injury file was active for that day
2. when the replay captured it
3. whether the share-matrix lookup was built against the same day’s out list

That also explains why some reruns can look very different even when the snapshot is the same. The player pool and support paths depend on the day’s invalidations, not just the game slate.

The good news is the latest rerun now appears to be reading the day-specific IAEL data correctly. The remaining question is no longer “was the injury report loaded,” but “why does a given day’s out list produce support for some legs and not others.”

User: Yes, the rerun has to have that time specific injury report pdf to go along with it. I do have all those that get produced during live runs so the injury report gets read. Its not just the day it is exactly that time report. The injury updates come out every 15 minutes thats why the pdfs read in 15 minute increments

GitHub Copilot: That makes sense, and it is an important distinction.

So the correct comparison unit is:

- the exact replay snapshot
- the exact injury-report PDF from that timestamp
- not just the calendar day

Because the updates come in 15-minute increments, a run at 2:15 and a run at 2:30 can legitimately see different IAEL support even if the slate is the same. That means the support matrix can change within the same day as the PDF changes.

So when we evaluate the rerun, we need to make sure it is paired with the same timestamped injury PDF that was live at that moment. If that file is different, the support result can be different even when the rest of the run looks similar.

That also means the latest rerun being “accurate” depends on whether it used the matching 15-minute injury update for that run time.

User: Exactly

GitHub Copilot: Yes. The replay only means anything when the snapshot and the exact 15-minute injury PDF match. If that timestamped report is different, IAEL support can legitimately change even on the same slate.

So the right comparison is: same snapshot, same run time, same injury-report PDF.

User: Correct

GitHub Copilot: Correct. Same snapshot, same timestamped injury PDF, and same run time are the only fair comparison. If any of those differ, the support output can legitimately change.

The blocker is now pinned to the share matrix, not the replay harness or IAEL outs.

- The Julius Randle REB leg uses the same out list in both runs: `["beringer joan", "anthony edwards", "enrique freeman", "julian phillips", "pullin zyon"]`.
- Control still had support: `role_ctx_reason = ok`, `role_ctx_outs_used = 1`, and `role_ctx_by_out` included Anthony Edwards.
- Current lost it completely: `role_ctx_reason = no_beneficiary_match`, `role_ctx_outs_used = 0`.
- The exact row changed in the matrix itself:
  - Older backup share_matrix.csv.OFF: `MIN,Anthony Edwards,Julius Randle,REB,10,0.04684904351659703`
  - Current share_matrix.csv: `MIN,Anthony Edwards,Julius Randle,REB,11,0.0`
- That means the regression came from a newer matrix build or source snapshot, not from the runtime combo logic.
- The current matrix file is also newer on disk than the backup, so this is consistent with a rebuild after the control run.

Next step I’d take:
1. Diff the inputs that fed the 2026-03-19 matrix rebuild, especially nba_gamelogs.csv and the build window in build_share_matrix.py.
2. Trace whether the Anthony Edwards -> Julius Randle REB row was zeroed by source-data drift or by a change in `build_removed_share_matrix` in team_share_reallocator.py.

- It is already validated against the current rerun.
- Its Julius Randle REB path still had support.
- The same out list is present in both runs, so the loss is in the matrix artifact, not replay plumbing.

The seam is now clear:
- Older matrix backup: share_matrix.csv.OFF has `MIN,Anthony Edwards,Julius Randle,REB,10,0.04684904351659703`
- Current matrix: share_matrix.csv has `MIN,Anthony Edwards,Julius Randle,REB,11,0.0`

That means the bottleneck is the share-matrix build path, not the reader/runtime path.

Completed with input: {
  "workspaceRoot": "c:\\Users\\rick\\projects\\Atlas",
  "workingDirectory": "c:\\Users\\rick\\projects\\Atlas",
  "timeout": 120000,
  "codeSnippet": "import pandas as pd\nfrom pathlib import Path\n\nroot = Path(r'c:/Users/rick/projects/Atlas')\npaths = {\n    'current': root / 'data/model/share_matrix.csv',\n    'backup': root / 'data/model/share_matrix.csv.OFF',\n}\n\ndef load(path):\n    df = pd.read_csv(path)\n    for c in ['team', 'out_player', 'beneficiary_player', 'stat']:\n        df[c] = df[c].astype(str).str.strip()\n    df['weight'] = pd.to_numeric(df['weight'], errors='coerce').fillna(0.0)\n    df['games'] = pd.to_numeric(df['games'], errors='coerce').fillna(0).astype(int)\n    return df\n\ncur = load(paths['current'])\nbak = load(paths['backup'])\n\nfor name, df in [('current', cur), ('backup', bak)]:\n    print('\\n##', name)\n    print('rows', len(df))\n    print('nonzero rows', int((df['weight'] > 0).sum()))\n    print('zero rows', int((df['weight'] == 0).sum()))\n    print('teams', df['team'].nunique())\n    print('outs', df['out_player'].nunique())\n    print('beneficiaries', df['beneficiary_player'].nunique())\n    print('stats', df['stat'].nunique())\n    print('avg games', round(df['games'].mean(), 3))\n    print('median games', int(df['games'].median()))\n    print('pct nonzero', round((df['weight'] > 0).mean(), 4))\n\nkey_cols = ['team', 'out_player', 'beneficiary_player', 'stat']\nmerged = cur[key_cols + ['games', 'weight']].merge(\n    bak[key_cols + ['games', 'weight']], on=key_cols, how='outer', suffixes=('_cur', '_bak'), indicator=True\n)\nprint('\\n## merge')\nprint('only current', int((merged['_merge'] == 'left_only').sum()))\nprint('only backup', int((merged['_merge'] == 'right_only').sum()))\nprint('both', int((merged['_merge'] == 'both').sum()))\n\nboth = merged[merged['_merge'] == 'both'].copy()\nboth['weight_diff'] = both['weight_cur'] - both['weight_bak']\nboth['abs_diff'] = both['weight_diff'].abs()\nprint('rows with weight diff > 0.001', int((both['abs_diff'] > 0.001).sum()))\nprint('rows with current zero and backup positive', int(((both['weight_cur'] == 0) & (both['weight_bak'] > 0)).sum()))\nprint('rows with current positive and backup zero', int(((both['weight_cur'] > 0) & (both['weight_bak'] == 0)).sum()))\nprint('top 10 drops')\nprint(both.sort_values('weight_diff').head(10)[key_cols + ['games_cur', 'weight_cur', 'games_bak', 'weight_bak']].to_string(index=False))\n"
}

Ran `pylanceRunCodeSnippet` 
Completed with input: {
  "workspaceRoot": "c:\\Users\\rick\\projects\\Atlas",
  "workingDirectory": "c:\\Users\\rick\\projects\\Atlas",
  "timeout": 120000,
  "codeSnippet": "import pandas as pd\nfrom pathlib import Path\n\nroot = Path(r'c:/Users/rick/projects/Atlas')\ndf = pd.read_csv(root / 'data/gamelogs/nba_gamelogs.csv')\n\n# Recreate the builder's relevant pieces for MIN / Anthony Edwards -> Julius Randle / REB.\ndf['game_date'] = pd.to_datetime(df['game_date'], errors='coerce').dt.normalize()\ndf = df.dropna(subset=['game_date']).copy()\ndf['team'] = df['team'].astype(str).str.upper().str.strip()\ndf['player'] = df['player'].astype(str).str.strip()\nfor c in ['minutes', 'pts', 'reb', 'ast']:\n    df[c] = pd.to_numeric(df[c], errors='coerce').fillna(0.0)\n\ncut = df['game_date'].max() - pd.Timedelta(days=140)\ndf = df[df['game_date'] >= cut].copy()\ndf['active'] = df['minutes'] > 0\n\nteam_date = df.groupby(['team', 'game_date'], as_index=False).agg(\n    team_pts=('pts', 'sum'),\n    team_reb=('reb', 'sum'),\n    team_ast=('ast', 'sum'),\n    team_min=('minutes', 'sum'),\n)\ndf = df.merge(team_date, on=['team', 'game_date'], how='left')\nfor fam in ['pts', 'reb', 'ast', 'min']:\n    df[f'{fam}_share'] = df[fam if fam != 'min' else 'minutes'] / df[f'team_{fam}'].replace({0.0: pd.NA})\n    df[f'{fam}_share'] = df[f'{fam}_share'].fillna(0.0)\n\nbase = df[df['active']].groupby(['team', 'player'], as_index=False).agg(\n    base_pts_share=('pts_share', 'mean'),\n    base_reb_share=('reb_share', 'mean'),\n    base_ast_share=('ast_share', 'mean'),\n    base_min_share=('min_share', 'mean'),\n    games=('active', 'sum'),\n    avg_min=('minutes', 'mean'),\n)\nbase_rot = base[(base['games'] >= 6) & (base['avg_min'] >= 8.0)].copy()\nrotation_players = base_rot.groupby('team')['player'].apply(set).to_dict()\n\nactive_players = (\n    df[df['active']]\n    .groupby(['team', 'game_date'])['player']\n    .apply(list)\n    .reset_index(name='active_players')\n)\n\nouts_rows = []\nfor _, r in active_players.iterrows():\n    if r['team'] != 'MIN':\n        continue\n    active_set = set(r['active_players'])\n    rotation = rotation_players.get('MIN', set())\n    for out_p in rotation - active_set:\n        if out_p == 'Anthony Edwards':\n            outs_rows.append((r['game_date'], out_p))\nouts_df = pd.DataFrame(outs_rows, columns=['game_date', 'out_player'])\nobs = df[(df['active']) & (df['team'] == 'MIN')][['team', 'game_date', 'player', 'pts_share', 'reb_share', 'ast_share']].rename(columns={'player': 'beneficiary_player'})\nbase_ben = base.rename(columns={'player': 'beneficiary_player'})\nobs = obs.merge(base_ben, on=['team', 'beneficiary_player'], how='left')\nobs['d_reb'] = obs['reb_share'] - obs['base_reb_share']\njoined = outs_df.merge(obs[['team', 'game_date', 'beneficiary_player', 'd_reb']], on=['game_date'], how='left')\njoined = joined[joined['beneficiary_player'] == 'Julius Randle'].copy()\nprint('games', len(joined))\nprint(joined[['game_date', 'd_reb']].to_string(index=False))\nprint('positive', int((joined['d_reb'] > 0).sum()), 'zero', int((joined['d_reb'] == 0).sum()), 'negative', int((joined['d_reb'] < 0).sum()))\nprint('mean', joined['d_reb'].mean(), 'max', joined['d_reb'].max(), 'min', joined['d_reb'].min())\n"
}

  "codeSnippet": "import pandas as pd\nfrom pathlib import Path\nfrom Atlas.model.team_share_reallocator import build_removed_share_matrix\n\nroot = Path(r'c:/Users/rick/projects/Atlas')\ndf = pd.read_csv(root / 'data/gamelogs/nba_gamelogs.csv')\ndf['game_date'] = pd.to_datetime(df['game_date'], errors='coerce').dt.normalize()\ndf = df.dropna(subset=['game_date']).copy()\ndf['team'] = df['team'].astype(str).str.upper().str.strip()\ndf['player'] = df['player'].astype(str).str.strip()\nfor c in ['minutes', 'pts', 'reb', 'ast']:\n    df[c] = pd.to_numeric(df[c], errors='coerce').fillna(0.0)\ncut = df['game_date'].max() - pd.Timedelta(days=140)\ndf = df[df['game_date'] >= cut].copy()\ndf['active'] = df['minutes'] > 0\nteam_date = df.groupby(['team', 'game_date'], as_index=False).agg(team_pts=('pts', 'sum'), team_reb=('reb', 'sum'), team_ast=('ast', 'sum'), team_min=('minutes', 'sum'))\ndf = df.merge(team_date, on=['team', 'game_date'], how='left')\ndf['pts_share'] = (df['pts'] / df['team_pts'].replace({0.0: pd.NA})).fillna(0.0)\ndf['reb_share'] = (df['reb'] / df['team_reb'].replace({0.0: pd.NA})).fillna(0.0)\ndf['ast_share'] = (df['ast'] / df['team_ast'].replace({0.0: pd.NA})).fillna(0.0)\ndf['min_share'] = (df['minutes'] / df['team_min'].replace({0.0: pd.NA})).fillna(0.0)\nbase = df[df['active']].groupby(['team', 'player'], as_index=False).agg(base_pts_share=('pts_share', 'mean'), base_reb_share=('reb_share', 'mean'), base_ast_share=('ast_share', 'mean'), base_min_share=('min_share', 'mean'), games=('active', 'sum'), avg_min=('minutes', 'mean'))\nbase_rot = base[(base['games'] >= 6) & (base['avg_min'] >= 8.0)].copy()\nrotation_players = base_rot.groupby('team')['player'].apply(set).to_dict()\nactive_players = df[df['active']].groupby(['team', 'game_date'])['player'].apply(list).reset_index(name='active_players')\nouts_rows = []\nfor _, r in active_players.iterrows():\n    if r['team'] != 'MIN':\n        continue\n    active_set = set(r['active_players'])\n    rotation = rotation_players.get('MIN', set())\n    missing_players = rotation - active_set\n    for out_p in missing_players:\n        outs_rows.append((r['game_date'], out_p))\nouts_df = pd.DataFrame(outs_rows, columns=['team_date', 'out_player'])\nobs = df[df['active'] & (df['team'] == 'MIN')][['team', 'game_date', 'player', 'pts_share', 'reb_share', 'ast_share']].rename(columns={'player': 'beneficiary_player'})\nbase_ben = base.rename(columns={'player': 'beneficiary_player'})\nobs = obs.merge(base_ben, on=['team', 'beneficiary_player'], how='left')\nobs['d_reb'] = obs['reb_share'] - obs['base_reb_share']\njoined = outs_df.merge(obs[['team', 'game_date', 'beneficiary_player', 'd_reb']], left_on=['team_date'], right_on=['game_date'], how='left')\njoined = joined[joined['out_player'] == 'Anthony Edwards'].copy()\njoined = joined[joined['beneficiary_player'] == 'Julius Randle'].copy()\nagg = joined.groupby(['team', 'out_player', 'beneficiary_player'], as_index=False).agg(delta_share=('d_reb', 'mean'), games=('d_reb', 'size'))\nagg['pos_delta'] = agg['delta_share'].clip(lower=0.0)\nagg['sum_pos'] = agg['pos_delta'].sum()\nagg['weight'] = agg['pos_delta'] / agg['sum_pos'] if agg['sum_pos'].iloc[0] > 0 else 0.0\nprint('manual agg row')\nprint(agg.to_string(index=False))\n\nmat = build_removed_share_matrix(df.rename(columns={'game_date':'game_date', 'player':'player', 'minutes':'minutes', 'pts':'pts', 'reb':'reb', 'ast':'ast'}), recent_days=140, min_rotation_games=6, min_rotation_avg_min=8.0)\nrow = mat[(mat['team'] == 'MIN') & (mat['out_player'] == 'Anthony Edwards') & (mat['beneficiary_player'] == 'Julius Randle') & (mat['stat'] == 'REB')]\nprint('\\nbuilder row')\nprint(row.to_string(index=False))\n"
}

I found the actual seam: the builder averages signed `delta_share` first, then clips negatives to zero. That means a mixed row with several positive games can still collapse to zero if the negative games dominate the mean. I’m simulating a positive-only aggregation to measure the density tradeoff before I patch it.

Ran `pylanceRunCodeSnippet` 
Completed with input: {
  "workspaceRoot": "c:\\Users\\rick\\projects\\Atlas",
  "workingDirectory": "c:\\Users\\rick\\projects\\Atlas",
  "timeout": 120000,
  "codeSnippet": "import pandas as pd\nfrom pathlib import Path\n\nroot = Path(r'c:/Users/rick/projects/Atlas')\ndf = pd.read_csv(root / 'data/gamelogs/nba_gamelogs.csv')\ndf['game_date'] = pd.to_datetime(df['game_date'], errors='coerce').dt.normalize()\ndf = df.dropna(subset=['game_date']).copy()\ndf['team'] = df['team'].astype(str).str.upper().str.strip()\ndf['player'] = df['player'].astype(str).str.strip()\nfor c in ['minutes', 'pts', 'reb', 'ast']:\n    df[c] = pd.to_numeric(df[c], errors='coerce').fillna(0.0)\ncut = df['game_date'].max() - pd.Timedelta(days=140)\ndf = df[df['game_date'] >= cut].copy()\ndf['active'] = df['minutes'] > 0\nteam_date = df.groupby(['team', 'game_date'], as_index=False).agg(team_pts=('pts', 'sum'), team_reb=('reb', 'sum'), team_ast=('ast', 'sum'), team_min=('minutes', 'sum'))\ndf = df.merge(team_date, on=['team', 'game_date'], how='left')\ndf['pts_share'] = (df['pts'] / df['team_pts'].replace({0.0: pd.NA})).fillna(0.0)\ndf['reb_share'] = (df['reb'] / df['team_reb'].replace({0.0: pd.NA})).fillna(0.0)\ndf['ast_share'] = (df['ast'] / df['team_ast'].replace({0.0: pd.NA})).fillna(0.0)\ndf['min_share'] = (df['minutes'] / df['team_min'].replace({0.0: pd.NA})).fillna(0.0)\nbase = df[df['active']].groupby(['team', 'player'], as_index=False).agg(base_pts_share=('pts_share', 'mean'), base_reb_share=('reb_share', 'mean'), base_ast_share=('ast_share', 'mean'), base_min_share=('min_share', 'mean'), games=('active', 'sum'), avg_min=('minutes', 'mean'))\nbase_rot = base[(base['games'] >= 6) & (base['avg_min'] >= 8.0)].copy()\nrotation_players = base_rot.groupby('team')['player'].apply(set).to_dict()\nactive_players = df[df['active']].groupby(['team', 'game_date'])['player'].apply(list).reset_index(name='active_players')\n\nouts_rows = []\nfor _, r in active_players.iterrows():\n    team = r['team']\n    active_set = set(r['active_players'])\n    rotation = rotation_players.get(team, set())\n    missing_players = rotation - active_set\n    for out_p in missing_players:\n        outs_rows.append((team, r['game_date'], out_p))\nouts_df = pd.DataFrame(outs_rows, columns=['team', 'game_date', 'out_player'])\nobs = df[df['active']][['team', 'game_date', 'player', 'pts_share', 'reb_share', 'ast_share']].rename(columns={'player': 'beneficiary_player'})\nbase_ben = base.rename(columns={'player': 'beneficiary_player'})\nobs = obs.merge(base_ben, on=['team', 'beneficiary_player'], how='left')\nobs['d_pts'] = obs['pts_share'] - obs['base_pts_share']\nobs['d_reb'] = obs['reb_share'] - obs['base_reb_share']\nobs['d_ast'] = obs['ast_share'] - obs['base_ast_share']\njoined = outs_df.merge(obs[['team', 'game_date', 'beneficiary_player', 'd_pts', 'd_reb', 'd_ast']], on=['team', 'game_date'], how='left')\njoined = joined[joined['out_player'] != joined['beneficiary_player']].copy()\n\n# Baseline (current logic): mean signed delta, clip negative to zero.\ncurrent_rows = []\nfor stat, col in [('PTS','d_pts'), ('REB','d_reb'), ('AST','d_ast')]:\n    agg = joined.groupby(['team', 'out_player', 'beneficiary_player'], as_index=False).agg(delta=(col, 'mean'), games=(col, 'size'))\n    agg['stat'] = stat\n    agg['pos'] = agg['delta'].clip(lower=0.0)\n    agg['sum_pos'] = agg.groupby(['team', 'out_player'])['pos'].transform('sum')\n    agg['weight'] = agg['pos'] / agg['sum_pos']\n    current_rows.append(agg[['team','out_player','beneficiary_player','stat','games','weight']])\ncurrent = pd.concat(current_rows, ignore_index=True)\ncurrent['weight'] = current['weight'].fillna(0.0)\n\n# Hypothetical: mean of positive deltas, then normalize.\nalt_rows = []\nfor stat, col in [('PTS','d_pts'), ('REB','d_reb'), ('AST','d_ast')]:\n    tmp = joined.copy()\n    tmp['pos_delta'] = tmp[col].clip(lower=0.0)\n    agg = tmp.groupby(['team', 'out_player', 'beneficiary_player'], as_index=False).agg(score=('pos_delta', 'mean'), games=('pos_delta', 'size'))\n    agg['stat'] = stat\n    agg['sum_score'] = agg.groupby(['team', 'out_player'])['score'].transform('sum')\n    agg['weight'] = agg['score'] / agg['sum_score']\n    alt_rows.append(agg[['team','out_player','beneficiary_player','stat','games','weight']])\nalt = pd.concat(alt_rows, ignore_index=True)\nalt['weight'] = alt['weight'].fillna(0.0)\n\nfor name, mat in [('current', current), ('alt_posmean', alt)]:\n    print('\\n##', name)\n    print('rows', len(mat), 'nonzero', int((mat['weight'] > 0).sum()), 'pct_nonzero', round(float((mat['weight'] > 0).mean()), 4))\n    target = mat[(mat['team'] == 'MIN') & (mat['out_player'] == 'Anthony Edwards') & (mat['beneficiary_player'] == 'Julius Randle') & (mat['stat'] == 'REB')]\n    print('target weight', None if target.empty else float(target.iloc[0]['weight']))\n    print('MIN/Julius REB positive rows', int(((mat['team'] == 'MIN') & (mat['beneficiary_player'] == 'Julius Randle') & (mat['stat'] == 'REB') & (mat['weight'] > 0)).sum()))\n    print('MIN/Julius REB total rows', int(((mat['team'] == 'MIN') & (mat['beneficiary_player'] == 'Julius Randle') & (mat['stat'] == 'REB')).sum()))\n"
}

The pure positive-mean version is too dense, so I’m narrowing to a thresholded floor: enough positive evidence to keep a row alive, but not so much that we light up nearly every beneficiary. I’m measuring the positive/negative game balance per row to choose that threshold.

pd.Timedelta(days=140)\ndf = df[df['game_date'] >= cut].copy()\ndf['active'] = df['minutes'] > 0\nteam_date = df.groupby(['team', 'game_date'], as_index=False).agg(team_pts=('pts', 'sum'), team_reb=('reb', 'sum'), team_ast=('ast', 'sum'), team_min=('minutes', 'sum'))\ndf = df.merge(team_date, on=['team', 'game_date'], how='left')\ndf['reb_share'] = (df['reb'] / df['team_reb'].replace({0.0: pd.NA})).fillna(0.0)\nbase = df[df['active']].groupby(['team', 'player'], as_index=False).agg(base_reb_share=('reb_share', 'mean'), games=('active', 'sum'), avg_min=('minutes', 'mean'))\nbase_rot = base[(base['games'] >= 6) & (base['avg_min'] >= 8.0)].copy()\nrotation_players = base_rot.groupby('team')['player'].apply(set).to_dict()\nactive_players = df[df['active']].groupby(['team', 'game_date'])['player'].apply(list).reset_index(name='active_players')\nouts_rows = []\nfor _, r in active_players.iterrows():\n    if r['team'] != 'MIN':\n        continue\n    missing_players = rotation_players.get('MIN', set()) - set(r['active_players'])\n    for out_p in missing_players:\n        if out_p == 'Anthony Edwards':\n            outs_rows.append((r['game_date'], out_p))\nouts_df = pd.DataFrame(outs_rows, columns=['game_date', 'out_player'])\nobs = df[df['active'] & (df['team'] == 'MIN')][['team', 'game_date', 'player', 'reb_share']].rename(columns={'player': 'beneficiary_player'})\nbase_ben = base.rename(columns={'player': 'beneficiary_player'})\nobs = obs.merge(base_ben, on=['team', 'beneficiary_player'], how='left')\nobs['d_reb'] = obs['reb_share'] - obs['base_reb_share']\njoined = outs_df.merge(obs[['team', 'game_date', 'beneficiary_player', 'd_reb']], on=['game_date'], how='left')\njoined = joined[joined['beneficiary_player'].notna()].copy()\n\n# Group stats for REB only on MIN/Anthony Edwards.\ng = joined.groupby(['team', 'out_player', 'beneficiary_player'], as_index=False).agg(\n    delta_mean=('d_reb', 'mean'),\n    pos_mean=('d_reb', lambda s: s[s > 0].mean() if (s > 0).any() else 0.0),\n    pos_hits=('d_reb', lambda s: int((s > 0).sum())),\n    neg_hits=('d_reb', lambda s: int((s < 0).sum())),\n    games=('d_reb', 'size'),\n)\nprint('target row:')\nprint(g[(g['team'] == 'MIN') & (g['out_player'] == 'Anthony Edwards') & (g['beneficiary_player'] == 'Julius Randle')].to_string(index=False))\n\n# Distribution of mixed rows with negative mean but positive hits.\nnegpos = g[(g['delta_mean'] <= 0) & (g['pos_hits'] > 0)].copy()\nprint('\\nrows with neg/zero mean and positive hits:', len(negpos))\nfor k in [1,2,3,4,5,6]:\n    print(f'pos_hits >= {k}:', int((negpos['pos_hits'] >= k).sum()))\nprint('\\nTop rows by positive hits among neg/zero mean rows:')\nprint(negpos.sort_values(['pos_hits','pos_mean'], ascending=False).head(15).to_string(index=False))\n"
}

The target row is unusually clean: only three MIN REB rows have mixed evidence with a nonpositive mean, and Julius Randle is the strongest of them. I’m checking the whole matrix to see how many rows would be affected by a hit-count floor, so we can keep the fix narrow.

pd.Timedelta(days=140)\ndf = df[df['game_date'] >= cut].copy()\ndf['active'] = df['minutes'] > 0\nteam_date = df.groupby(['team', 'game_date'], as_index=False).agg(team_pts=('pts', 'sum'), team_reb=('reb', 'sum'), team_ast=('ast', 'sum'), team_min=('minutes', 'sum'))\ndf = df.merge(team_date, on=['team', 'game_date'], how='left')\nfor fam, col in [('pts','pts'), ('reb','reb'), ('ast','ast')]:\n    df[f'{fam}_share'] = (df[col] / df[f'team_{fam}'].replace({0.0: pd.NA})).fillna(0.0)\nbase = df[df['active']].groupby(['team', 'player'], as_index=False).agg(base_pts_share=('pts_share', 'mean'), base_reb_share=('reb_share', 'mean'), base_ast_share=('ast_share', 'mean'), games=('active', 'sum'), avg_min=('minutes', 'mean'))\nbase_rot = base[(base['games'] >= 6) & (base['avg_min'] >= 8.0)].copy()\nrotation_players = base_rot.groupby('team')['player'].apply(set).to_dict()\nactive_players = df[df['active']].groupby(['team', 'game_date'])['player'].apply(list).reset_index(name='active_players')\nouts_rows = []\nfor _, r in active_players.iterrows():\n    rotation = rotation_players.get(r['team'], set())\n    missing_players = rotation - set(r['active_players'])\n    for out_p in missing_players:\n        outs_rows.append((r['team'], r['game_date'], out_p))\nouts_df = pd.DataFrame(outs_rows, columns=['team', 'game_date', 'out_player'])\nobs = df[df['active']][['team', 'game_date', 'player', 'pts_share', 'reb_share', 'ast_share']].rename(columns={'player': 'beneficiary_player'})\nbase_ben = base.rename(columns={'player': 'beneficiary_player'})\nobs = obs.merge(base_ben, on=['team', 'beneficiary_player'], how='left')\nfor col, base_col in [('pts_share','base_pts_share'), ('reb_share','base_reb_share'), ('ast_share','base_ast_share')]:\n    obs[f'd_{col.split(\"_\")[0]}'] = obs[col] - obs[base_col]\njoined = outs_df.merge(obs[['team','game_date','beneficiary_player','d_pts','d_reb','d_ast']], on=['team','game_date'], how='left')\njoined = joined[joined['beneficiary_player'].notna()].copy()\n\nfor stat in ['pts', 'reb', 'ast']:\n    col = f'd_{stat}'\n    g = joined.groupby(['team','out_player','beneficiary_player'], as_index=False).agg(\n        delta_mean=(col,'mean'),\n        pos_hits=(col, lambda s: int((s > 0).sum())),\n        games=(col,'size')\n    )\n    negpos = g[(g['delta_mean'] <= 0) & (g['pos_hits'] > 0)].copy()\n    print('\\n##', stat.upper())\n    print('mixed rows', len(negpos), 'of', len(g))\n    for k in [1,2,3,4,5]:\n        print(f'pos_hits >= {k}:', int((negpos['pos_hits'] >= k).sum()))\n    print('top mixed rows')\n    print(negpos.sort_values(['pos_hits','delta_mean'], ascending=[False, True]).head(10).to_string(index=False))\n"
}

The hit-count floor is too blunt, so I’m trying a shrinkage blend instead: signed mean plus a small fraction of positive-only mean. That should rescue the target edge without turning nearly every mixed row positive.

pd.Timedelta(days=140)\ndf = df[df['game_date'] >= cut].copy()\ndf['active'] = df['minutes'] > 0\nteam_date = df.groupby(['team', 'game_date'], as_index=False).agg(team_pts=('pts', 'sum'), team_reb=('reb', 'sum'), team_ast=('ast', 'sum'), team_min=('minutes', 'sum'))\ndf = df.merge(team_date, on=['team', 'game_date'], how='left')\ndf['pts_share'] = (df['pts'] / df['team_pts'].replace({0.0: pd.NA})).fillna(0.0)\ndf['reb_share'] = (df['reb'] / df['team_reb'].replace({0.0: pd.NA})).fillna(0.0)\ndf['ast_share'] = (df['ast'] / df['team_ast'].replace({0.0: pd.NA})).fillna(0.0)\nbase = df[df['active']].groupby(['team', 'player'], as_index=False).agg(base_pts_share=('pts_share', 'mean'), base_reb_share=('reb_share', 'mean'), base_ast_share=('ast_share', 'mean'), games=('active', 'sum'), avg_min=('minutes', 'mean'))\nbase_rot = base[(base['games'] >= 6) & (base['avg_min'] >= 8.0)].copy()\nrotation_players = base_rot.groupby('team')['player'].apply(set).to_dict()\nactive_players = df[df['active']].groupby(['team', 'game_date'])['player'].apply(list).reset_index(name='active_players')\nouts_rows = []\nfor _, r in active_players.iterrows():\n    missing_players = rotation_players.get(r['team'], set()) - obs['base_pts_share']\nobs['d_reb'] = obs['reb_share'] - obs['base_reb_share']\nobs['d_ast'] = obs['ast_share'] - obs['base_ast_share']\njoined = outs_df.merge(obs[['team','game_date','beneficiary_player','d_pts','d_reb','d_ast']], on=['team','game_date'], how='left')\njoined = joined[joined['beneficiary_player'].notna()].copy()\n\nfor lam in [0.1, 0.15, 0.2, 0.25, 0.3, 0.4]:\n    print(f'\\n## lambda={lam}')\n    for stat in ['pts','reb','ast']:\n        col = f'd_{stat}'\n        g = joined.groupby(['team','out_player','beneficiary_player'], as_index=False).agg(\n            signed_mean=(col,'mean'),\n            pos_mean=(col, lambda s: s[s > 0].mean() if (s > 0).any() else 0.0),\n            games=(col,'size')\n        )\n        g['score'] = g['signed_mean'] + lam * g['pos_mean']\n        g['score'] = g['score'].clip(lower=0.0)\n        g['sum_score'] = g.groupby(['team','out_player'])['score'].transform('sum')\n        g['weight'] = g['score'] / g['sum_score']\n        nz = int((g['weight'] > 0).sum())\n        target = g[(g['team'] == 'MIN') & (g['out_player'] == 'Anthony Edwards') & (g['beneficiary_player'] == 'Julius Randle')]\n        tw = None if target.empty else float(target.iloc[0]['weight'])\n        print(stat.upper(), 'nonzero', nz, 'target', tw)\n"
}
